description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the ubuntu:20.04 Docker image with a global before_script that runs `bash ci/docker_install.sh` and three stages: test, deploy, build. In the build stage add a `build_nightly` job that runs only on the `build` ref, executes `npm install -g npm`, `make`, `version=nightly make appstore`, and `mv /tmp/build/gpxpod-nightly.tar.gz ./`, then stores `gpxpod-nightly.tar.gz` as an artifact. In the test stage add three jobs—`test_nc_mysql`, `test_nc_pgsql`, and `test_nc_sqlite`—each runs on `master` or `test` refs; each clones the Nextcloud server repo, patches `nextcloud/lib/autoloader.php`, copies the gpxpod source, installs the app with `occ`, runs `jshint --verbose gpxpod/js/gpxpod.js`, creates a `coverage` directory, and runs `phpunit` with the appropriate configuration (the sqlite job also generates HTML coverage and defines a coverage regex). The `test_nc_mysql` job installs `mariadb-server` and `php7.4-mysql`, starts MySQL, creates a test database and user; the `test_nc_pgsql` job installs `php7.4-pgsql` and `postgresql-client`, uses a `postgres:latest` service with `POSTGRES_DB`, `POSTGRES_USER`, and `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` variables; the sqlite job uses no external service. Finally, in the deploy stage add a `pages` job that runs only on the `master` branch, overrides the before_script with `echo 'nothing to install for pages jobs'`, depends on `test_nc_sqlite`, sleeps 5 seconds, creates a `public` directory, moves the `coverage` artifact into `public`, and publishes the `public/` directory as a GitLab Pages artifact.
